name: Miquelita's Tortillas (Flour)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the white flour, wheat flour, baking powder, and salt. Mix well.
2. Add the oil, milk, and warm water. Stir until all ingredients are well blended and form a dough.
3. On a floured surface, knead the dough for 2 minutes.
4. Using oiled hands, shape the dough into twelve 2-inch balls.
5. Roll each ball into a 1/8-inch thick circle.
6. Place the tortillas on a heated grill. Cook each side for 2 minutes or until cooked through.
7. Makes 1 dozen tortillas.
Served at Casa de Martinez, Los Ojos, New Mexico. The recipe was given to Clorinda Martinez de Sanchez, Innkeeper, by an employee (Ursula). It was her grandmother's. "In my opinion, these are the perfect tortillas!" says Mrs. Martinez de Sanchez.
Recipe from cookbook by Joan Stromquist, "Chile, Corn & Croissants, Delicious Recipes from New Mexico Inns"
